REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S HENRY PAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED; DEFENDANT HAS 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO ANSWER THE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT REPEATEDLY DISPUTES PLEADED FACTS, WHICH IS NOT THE FUNCTION OF A DEMURRER. FURTHER, DEFENDANT INSISTS ON A LEVEL OF DETAIL IN THE COMPLAINT THAT IS NOT REQUIRED IN CALIFORNIA, WHICH IS A NOTICE-PLEADING JURISDICTION. NOR DOES DEFENDANT CITE AUTHORITY FOR HIS NOTION THAT A "COMPLETE COPY OF THE LEASE" HAD TO BE ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT. FINALLY, A GENERAL DEMURRER DOES NOT LIE TO ONLY PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF EDUARDO TAY'S MOTION FOR VIOLATION OF COURT ORDER AND TO COMPEL SITE INSPECTION; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENSE COUNSEL SZE HANG LO AND SHL LAW GROUP

Eric Kim of the Hooshmand Law Group (415.318.5709) for plaintiff and moving party; No other appearances.

Discussed and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Site inspection to take place no later than August 5, 2016 on at least a 48-hour notice. Motion to Compel Deposition is Denied. It is not supported by sufficient evidence. See Kim declaration. Request for sanctions against Sze Hang Lo, Martin Guerbadot and SHL Law Group in the amount of $9,650 is GRANTED, no opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Set For Trial On Aug-08-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
EAric Kim, esq., (415)of Hooshmand Law Group for Plaintiff/Opposing party Eduardo Tay.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT'S HENRY PAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED; defendant has 10 days from notice of entry of order to answer the complaint. Defendant repeatedly disputes pleaded facts, which is not the function of a demurrer. Further, defendant insists on a level of detail in the complaint that is not required in California, which is a notice-pleading jurisdiction. Nor does defendant cite authority for his notion that a "complete copy of the lease" had to be attached to the complaint. Finally, a general demurrer does not lie to only part of a cause of action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
